evals.prompt: Create a Camunda 8 BPMN process that uses the AI Agent connector to handle customer support tickets. The agent should be able to look up customer data via a REST API and escalate to a human user task when needed.
evals.expected_output: Generates an ad-hoc subprocess with the AI Agent connector configured, a REST tool, and a user-task escalation tool
expectations: Uses bpmn:adHocSubProcess as the agent host element (not bpmn:subProcess)
Applies the AI Agent connector via c8ctl element-template apply
Models the customer lookup as a service task with no incoming sequence flow
Uses fromAi(toolCall.customerId, ...) inside the lookup tool's input mapping
Writes toolCallResult on every tool
Sets data.limits.maxModelCalls
Sets data.systemPrompt.prompt and data.userPrompt.prompt as FEEL strings (= prefix)

evals.prompt: Add a 'Search orders' tool to my AI agent ad-hoc subprocess. It calls GET https://api.example.com/orders?q=<query> with the query coming from the LLM, and returns the response body to the agent.
evals.expected_output: Adds a service task tool inside the ad-hoc subprocess with fromAi() for the query parameter and a resultExpression writing toolCallResult
expectations: Adds a serviceTask child of the ad-hoc subprocess with no incoming sequence flow
Names the tool clearly (e.g. 'Search orders') and includes bpmn:documentation
Uses fromAi(toolCall.query, ..., "string") to declare the LLM-supplied parameter
Sets resultExpression to ={toolCallResult: response.body}

evals.prompt: I need a tool that sends a chat message to the customer and waits for their reply before the agent continues. Model it.
evals.expected_output: Wraps a send service task + a webhook-correlated intermediate catch event in a sub-process tool
expectations: Wraps the send-then-wait pair in a bpmn:subProcess that acts as a single tool
Service task generates a unique correlationKey per invocation (e.g. chatId + '-' + string(now()))
Intermediate catch event uses inbound.type io.camunda:webhook:1 with correlationKeyExpression
Defines a bpmn:message with zeebe:subscription correlationKey pointing at the local variable
Maps the inbound payload into toolCallResult via resultExpression

evals.prompt: The agent should be able to take a follow-up message from the user after its first response and continue with the same conversation context. How do I wire that?
evals.expected_output: Explains feeding agent.context back in as agentContext on the next invocation
expectations: Pipes agent.context back in via <zeebe:input source="=agent.context" target="agentContext" /> on subsequent invocations
Uses a follow-up prompt branch (e.g. if is defined(followUpInput) then followUpInput else inputText)
Notes that data.response.includeAgentContext should be true
Writes the agent output back to a process variable so the next iteration can read it
